title: Parse On-Call Schedule
description: Given an on-call schedule and a query timestamp, determine which engineer is on call at that moment. The input consists of schedule lines (name,start_unix,end_unix) separated from the query timestamp by a line containing only '---'. An engineer is on call when start_unix <= query_time <= end_unix. If multiple entries match (e.g. at exact boundary), return the first one.
constraints: Query timestamp is a Unix integer on the line after '---'
Return the name of the on-call engineer or None if no one is scheduled
If multiple entries match, return the first one (earliest in file order)
Interval is closed: [start, end] inclusive on both ends

examples.explanation: Timestamp 1500 falls within alice's shift [1000, 2000]. Bob's shift starts at 2000 and carol's at 3000.

starter_code.python:
def find_oncall(schedule_and_query: str) -> str | None:
    pass

test_cases.description: Query timestamp falls within alice's shift
Query timestamp is outside all shifts, returns None
Query at boundary ts=2000 matches both alice and bob; first match (alice) is returned
